prompt: my wrists hurt after long coding sessions. looking for a split ergonomic keyboard, ideally under $250, that real people have used for a year plus and still recommend. not interested in whatever amazon is pushing
assertions: At least one source is a specialist community or long-term user report (forum, subreddit, enthusiast blog), not a commerce/SEO listicle
Each recommended product is supported by 2+ independent sources
Findings carry confidence levels and a Sources section
Addresses the longevity criterion (year-plus ownership signal) rather than launch reviews
